what_it_does: Defines a minimal non-commutative fan-in node type named pair that takes two Any inputs a and b and emits a single Text output pair formatted as '<a>><b>'.
observations: The docstring states the node is intentionally non-commutative so the test suite can exercise port-to-input binding in memo signatures.
PORTS_IN declares a and b as Any; PORTS_OUT declares pair as Text.
CONFIG is an empty dict and run() ignores the config argument.
run() uses inputs.get('a') and inputs.get('b'), defaulting to None if a key is missing.
standouts: The non-commutative design is the central signal; a commutative node would hide port-binding ordering bugs.
The implementation is a one-line skeleton with no editable configuration.
conventions: Follows a node-type substrate contract: VERSION, PORTS_IN, PORTS_OUT, CONFIG, and run(inputs, config).
Uses type hints in the run signature.
The docstring explains rationale and references an external design document (nodes/AGENTS.md).
concerns: run() does not validate that inputs contains the expected keys, so missing inputs silently produce strings like 'None><b' or 'None>None'.
CONFIG is declared but unused, which is intentional but could be mistaken for an incomplete implementation.
notes: A tiny, intentionally order-sensitive fan-in node used mainly to test port-binding correctness.
questions: What does the memo-signature test look like that requires a non-commutative node?
How does the runtime handle missing a or b inputs?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual behavior: it is a non-commutative fan-in node that emits '<a>><b>' and exists to expose port-binding order.
intention_signals: The file seems to be trying to provide the simplest possible order-sensitive fan-in node so that test infrastructure can verify that input values are bound to the correct named ports in memo signatures.
novelty: Distinct in that it deliberately avoids commutativity to make port-binding order visible; most fan-in nodes would not care about order.
contribution: Provides a deterministic, order-sensitive two-input node substrate that a fused system can keep as a regression fixture for port-to-input binding.
summary_for_embedding: A minimal non-commutative fan-in node type that emits a Text output '<a>><b>' from two Any inputs.
intention_for_embedding: Created as a skeleton node to exercise and verify correct port-to-input binding in memo signatures.

symbols.does: Returns the string '<a>><b>' from the a and b inputs.
symbols.description: The run function is the execution entry point for the pair node. It reads inputs['a'] and inputs['b'], ignores config, and returns a single Text output formatted so that input order is preserved and observable.
